preview_text:
The model rates the USA as a clear favorite at 66% to win, with Bosnia and Herzegovina holding a 9% chance of an upset. The gap reflects a substantial difference in expected quality, though the underdog's probability is not negligible.

The draw probability of 25% suggests a moderately competitive match. The win/loss split is wide enough that the USA enters as the likely victor, yet the model assigns meaningful probability to a stalemate, indicating the match is not heavily skewed toward decisive play.

Model and market are broadly aligned across all three outcomes. The sportsbook consensus places the USA at 69%, marginally higher than the model's 66%, and adjusts the draw down to 20% while raising Bosnia and Herzegovina to 11%. These gaps fall within typical market noise and do not indicate sharp disagreement. Polymarket prices align closely with the sportsbook view, suggesting consensus across pricing venues.
